skill.name: exa-search
skill.description: Semantic search, similar content discovery, and structured research using Exa API
skill.summary: Semantic search, similar content discovery, and structured research using Exa API
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were reviewed against the skill content. The npx command is an installation instruction, the URL is a documentation link, and the weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 33 are false positives with no cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: Installation Command Requires User Trust
low_findings.description: SKILL.md shows an npx command for installing a remote package. This is a common setup pattern, but users should review the target package before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is inside an installation code block and is not executed by the skill itself. It still asks users to run a remote package manager command.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Repository Link Is Documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md links to a GitHub repository for examples. No evidence found that the skill performs network requests or sends data to this URL.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is a normal markdown link. There is no executable fetch, webhook, credential transfer, or data exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static alerts at the description and troubleshooting lines do not reference cryptographic functions, algorithms, keys, hashes, or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found plain descriptive text and a troubleshooting sentence at the cited lines. No cryptographic implementation is present in the skill file.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Package Installation Instruction
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documentation includes npx installation guidance. This is not automatic execution, but it carries normal package supply chain risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is clearly presented for manual installation. Risk depends on the external package and user execution.

content.user_title: Find Research Sources with Exa Search
content.value_statement: Research tasks need relevant web results, similar sources, and category-focused discovery. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use Exa API workflows for semantic search.
actual_capabilities: Guides semantic search workflows that use the Exa API.
Supports finding similar web content from a known source.
Helps search category-focused sources such as companies, people, and research papers.
Provides setup guidance for configuring an Exa API key.
Directs users to the source repository for examples and troubleshooting.
limitations: Requires a separate Exa API key and valid account access.
Does not include executable integration code in this skill file.
Search quality depends on Exa API coverage and query wording.
Users must verify source accuracy before relying on results.

use_cases.title: Find Market Research Sources
use_cases.description: Use semantic search to discover companies, competitors, and relevant industry pages.

use_cases.title: Locate Related Technical Articles
use_cases.description: Find web pages similar to a known article or documentation page.

use_cases.title: Collect Academic Starting Points
use_cases.description: Search research paper categories and gather sources for deeper review.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Semantic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use exa-search to find high-quality sources about renewable energy storage trends. Summarize the most relevant themes.

prompt_templates.title: Find Similar Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Use exa-search to find pages similar to this article: [URL]. Group results by topic and explain why each group is relevant.

prompt_templates.title: Category Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Use exa-search to find companies working on privacy-preserving machine learning. Include short notes about each company.

prompt_templates.title: Structured Research Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use exa-search to research open source observability platforms. Compare major options, cite useful sources, and note gaps requiring verification.

output_examples.input: Find recent sources about AI search engines for enterprise teams.
output_examples.output: A grouped source list covering vendor pages, analysis articles, and technical explainers, with short relevance notes for each group.

output_examples.input: Find pages similar to a known product documentation page.
output_examples.output: A set of related documentation, comparison pages, and implementation guides, organized by how closely they match the original page.

output_examples.input: Search for research papers about retrieval augmented generation evaluation.
output_examples.output: A concise research starting list with paper titles, topic clusters, and follow-up questions for deeper review.

best_practices: Configure the Exa API key through environment variables.
Use specific queries that include topic, audience, and desired source type.
Validate important claims against primary sources before publishing.
anti_patterns: Do not treat search results as verified facts without review.
Do not share private or sensitive data in search queries.
Do not run installation commands before reviewing the referenced package.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps AI assistants use Exa search workflows for semantic search, similar content discovery, and structured research.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documentation says users should configure an Exa API key before use.

faq.question: Can it find similar pages?
faq.answer: Yes. Similar content discovery is one of the stated use cases in the skill documentation.

faq.question: Is this a full integration package?
faq.answer: No. The skill file provides guidance and references, but it does not include integration source code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk, mainly from manual installation guidance and an external documentation link.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
